        continue
    code = ''.join(cell['source'])
    exec(compile(code, f'cell_{idx}', 'exec'), ns)
print('NOTEBOOK_EXEC_OK')
PY

task: A private AI platform team has a 14-day commit window to decide whether to rent burst H100 capacity, sign a reservation, or delay internal model runs. The board cares about training-SLA misses, inference headroom, power draw, and stranded spend, not generic cloud-cost advice. removed context: NVIDIA DGX/HGX cluster design references, MLPerf-style accelerator throughput reporting, Kubernetes scheduling concepts, and public GPU cloud pricing/commitment patterns reviewed June 2026. Embedded operating packet: current cluster has 96 nodes, 8 H100-SXM GPUs per node, 80 GB per GPU, 42 TFLOP-normalized training units per GPU-hour, usable training pool after inference reservation=528 GPUs, maintenance outage=64 GPUs during days 5-6, network rule requires jobs over 256 GPUs to run in contiguous 8-GPU nodes with no more than 2 racks, power cap=640 kW, measured rack power=10.8 kW per 8-GPU node at training load, PUE=1.28, energy price=$0.118/kWh. Job backlog: J1 tokenizer-ablation 64 GPUs 18 hours value=$70,000 deadline day 3 preemptible=yes, J2 policy-model-SFT 128 GPUs 36 hours value=$240,000 deadline day 4 preemptible=no, J3 multimodal-pretrain 384 GPUs 92 hours value=$1,350,000 deadline day 10 preemptible=no, J4 eval-sweep 48 GPUs 20 hours value=$55,000 deadline day 7 preemptible=yes, J5 safety-redteam-generation 96 GPUs 44 hours value=$180,000 deadline day 9 preemptible=yes, J6 customer-finetune-batch 192 GPUs 54 hours value=$620,000 deadline day 12 preemptible=no, J7 embedding-refresh 32 GPUs 16 hours value=$40,000 deadline day 5 preemptible=yes. Capacity options: O1 on-demand H100=$4.85/GPU-hour available max 256 GPUs any day lead_time=4 hours, O2 30-day committed block=$3.28/GPU-hour for 256 GPUs with 80% take-or-pay and lead_time=24 hours, O3 colocated short lease 32 nodes=$2.45/GPU-hour plus $42,000 setup and 72-hour lead_time, O4 defer job penalty equals 35% of job value if missed by <=48 hours and 100% if later. Rules and constraints: compute feasible schedules in 6-hour buckets; nonpreemptible jobs must run continuously; preemptible jobs may split into at most 3 chunks with 2-hour restart overhead per split; training_pool plus rented GPUs cannot exceed power cap after PUE unless rented cloud capacity is offsite; objective=maximize delivered_job_value minus GPU rental/commit cost minus energy cost minus deadline penalties; reservation take-or-pay is charged even if unused; report utilization by owned, on-demand, committed, and colocated pools. Required work: deliver gpu_capacity_economics_optimizer.ipynb + capacity_board_memo.md with a schedule, option comparison, sensitivity to 20% throughput shortfall, stranded-commit exposure, power-cap evidence, and a board recommendation that can be audited from the embedded numbers only.
